meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching, start building. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to master product-led development and escape tutorial hell as a serious SaaS founder.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a symptom of passive consumption. To escape, pivot from following guides to solving specific, monetizable problems. Use AI-assisted scaffolding to generate your MVP architecture, iterate through high-frequency deployment cycles, and focus exclusively on shipping features that solve real-world user pain points rather than merely replicating tutorial code.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hundreds of hours watching polished videos, yet your GitHub contribution graph remains suspiciously barren. You are trapped in the high-definition illusion of progress—Tutorial Hell. It feels like learning, but it’s just procrastination disguised as professional development. The industry doesn't reward those who can replicate a Todo-App; it rewards those who can ship a functional, revenue-generating engine.

The shift from 'student' to 'founder' requires a brutal pivot: stop consuming, start building. By leveraging high-leverage tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can collapse months of development into days of execution. This isn't about learning syntax; it's about translating a business hypothesis into a live, scalable product that users can actually pay for.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing code, define the specific problem your MVP solves. Focus on one single feature that provides immediate utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Help me define a scope for an MVP that solves [Insert Problem] for [Insert Persona]. Create a lean feature list that can be built in under 48 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --prompt 'Generate a project spec for a SaaS that solves [Problem]'

steps.title: Scaffold the Architecture
steps.description: Use AI to set up your directory structure and core dependencies, ensuring your stack is production-ready, not tutorial-grade.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust, production-ready directory structure for a Next.js 14 app with TypeScript, Tailwind, and Supabase integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Scaffold the project with the generated directory structure and necessary config files'

steps.title: Implement the Core Logic
steps.description: Focus on the unique value add. Don't waste time on auth boilerplate; use existing services to get to the core logic faster.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the primary business logic for [Core Feature]. Ensure it is modular, tested, and handles edge cases for user input.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement the core business logic for [Feature] with proper error handling'

steps.title: Build the User Interface
steps.description: Convert your logic into a functional UI. Prioritize speed and clarity over aesthetic perfection for the first version.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, mobile-responsive UI component using Shadcn/UI for the [Core Feature] input and output flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude build 'Create the UI for the main user workflow using Shadcn components'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push to production immediately. Feedback from real users is the only metric that matters for a founder.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for production deployment, including environment variables, database migrations, and performance monitoring.

pro_tips: Never build an auth system from scratch; use Clerk or Supabase to save 20+ hours of development time.
Treat your codebase as a disposable asset; if it doesn't solve a user problem, delete it and pivot.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your core business logic; it’s the only way to ensure stability while moving fast.
Focus on 'Time to First Dollar'—prioritize payment integration over aesthetic design in your MVP.
Adopt a 'Ship-First, Refactor-Later' mindset; technical debt is a luxury you can afford only after you have your first paying customer.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, shift your focus to distribution. Spend 80% of your time on marketing and 20% on feature iteration based on direct user feedback.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of learning and start building for the sake of revenue. The tools are ready, the market is waiting, and your path to shipping is clear. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
